clean_body: Serving as your Attorney General has been the greatest honor of my life.| | | Friend, I love this job. Serving as Attorney General has been the greatest honor of my life. Every day, I wake up with a mission: to protect Virginia families, defend our values, and ensure justice is real for every single citizen. We’ve made serious progress: Virginia’s murder rate is down by 30%, and down 66% in Operation Ceasefire cities. Fentanyl deaths have dropped 48% because we treated it like the national security threat that it is. We’ve secured over $1.2 billion to fight the opioid crisis — the largest settlement in state history. But now, that progress is under threat. Jay Jones and Shannon Taylor are trying to bring their soft-on-crime, defund-the-police, anti-parent politics back to the Attorney General’s office. I won’t let that happen. Not on my watch. If you believe in what we’ve built—and the future is worth fighting for— I am not running to make headlines. I am running to protect your kids, your community, and your freedom. And with your support, we will win. In Service,Jason Miyares** Attorney General of Virginia The People's Protector | | | | | | ---|---|--- | | | | Paid for by Miyares for Virginia | | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com | | |

name: Kevin Roberts
subject: National Survey on Protecting Women’s Sports
body:
Please see a special message from our sponsor:

Unsubscribe
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xul=2qq8ny363wdaxf3eu1ztoj204itftqnup8brglu2pbcmjcvhm6w2wa73emktbwxff20g756u3n2iu2t4ulbxwe3q0wtrclf1ownzdygxq&__stmp=swqead&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh

Please see a special message from our sponsor:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=swqead&__onlt=t

Dear Fellow American,

I am enclosing a survey on a very urgent matter.

It will only take a moment of your time, but the topic is vital to our nation's future.

A movement is growing, led by progressives—but supported by many well-meaning Americans—to change the way we elect our president. In effect, it seeks to do away with the Electoral College as devised by the Framers of our Constitution.

That's why Hillsdale College is conducting a National Survey on Presidential Selection.

By completing this survey and returning it to me as soon as possible, you will help Hillsdale College more clearly understand the views of mainstream Americans concerning this issue—views we will make available to policymakers and opinion leaders.

You can complete the National Survey on Presidential Selection by using the link below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=17x64w94u6jditi0f3d34suh3n86xd6w61ycb7q6rkgcd7cucezzp3ab6mya30sof3s7jaa2gwuwd5024zuntfd8dio94zc8lpcti17pa7x0bpxnxtle1mcneiqoblvjy7lo5wo7tvjtmwvzqrce7owuwe771n&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=swqead&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=swqead&__onlt=t

Do you think that most Americans understand how the Electoral College works and its role in preserving free and representative government?

YES
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=swqead&__onlt=t

NO
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=swqead&__onlt=t

For most of our nation's history, the Electoral College, as devised by the Framers of our Constitution, was not controversial. Indeed, it was considered essential to preserving free and representative government.

But today, more than a dozen states have joined together in an attempt to undermine this constitutional bulwark of liberty.

This movement to undermine the Constitution has grown largely because of the failure of America's schools to provide young people with a proper grounding in American civics—too many Americans simply don't understand the importance of the Constitution, including the Electoral College, to liberty.

Because of this, Hillsdale College has expanded its educational mission on behalf of liberty nationwide, directly teaching millions of Americans in all 50 states.

These efforts, which aim to counter the Left's domination over most of our schools, include:

Teaching millions of citizens nationwide through free online courses, such as "Constitution 101" and "The Great American Story: A Land of Hope;"

Reaching over six million households and businesses nationwide with Imprimis, Hillsdale's free digest of liberty;

Promoting constitutional education through social media outlets popular with young people;

Sending millions of pocket-size copies of America's founding documents to citizens and K-12 schools across America; and

Restoring excellence to K-12 education by helping establish and guiding classical K-12 schools nationwide and providing, free of charge, Hillsdale's American classical K-12 curriculum (including American civics) to thousands of American educators and homeschoolers.

Your answers to the National Survey on Presidential Selection will help Hillsdale form the most effective strategy to educate millions of Americans about the importance of the Constitution to liberty—including the importance of the Electoral College.

And this is especially important in this critical time for America's future.

So please share your views on this urgent topic by completing our National Survey on Presidential Selection and returning it to me as soon as possible. It will only take a few minutes of your time.

You can take the survey using this secure link I've provided below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=17x64w94u6jditi0f3d34suh3n86xd6w61ipdzxkpfyzkzrb4rflkkuli8ohc6yi9xyd1q4a9yqtft0xwuwlo53otd5q8voh94rqb04nl7tdw8rfy4o82f19u3s9fy6j8ta9rtf1149cljo41rxfj227a34htv&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=swqead&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=swqead&__onlt=t

And after you take your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

Since 1844, Hillsdale has been "pursuing truth and defending liberty." Today, it does so through its extensive educational outreach efforts while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding—not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.

The support of private citizens like you is essential to our work.

Please represent your state and take the National Survey on Presidential Selection today. Thank you for your time and attention—and for your friendship and support of liberty.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn

President, Hillsdale College

Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

John

[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/NPP_Ed_logo_RGB_green1-01.jpg]
Hi Friend,
Welcome to the latest New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) newsletter!
Earlier this week, the PA [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] House State Government Committee [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] voted on two bills that have the potential to impact our voting rights: State Rep Mehaffie's Voter ID bill (HB771) and State Rep. McClinton's omnibus bill (HB1396). Both passed out of committee this week and will now be voted on by the full house.
An omnibus bill is a legislative bill that combines multiple, sometimes unrelated, provisions or bills into one single bill. It's essentially a package deal of different legislative proposals, often used to facilitate the passage of legislation, particularly appropriations bills.
HB 771 is sponsored by State Rep. Thomas Mehaffie (Dauphin) and “would require all voters arriving at their election precincts to show identification or attest under penalty of law that they are legally able to vote in Pennsylvania.”
State Rep. Malcolm Kenyatta (Philadelphia) voiced his opposition to the bill. The bill doesn’t address [https://www.politicspa.com/state-government-committee-sends-voter-id-election-omnibus-bills-to-house-floor/141399] the real issues at stake. “We need to solve problems of voter irregularities and voter fraud,” he said. “We do not have demonstrable evidence that leads us to make the point that we need to address voter ID in this way. Let’s talk about the issues that do exist. This legislation doesn’t address those.”
HB 1396 has a more recognizable name behind it – Speaker of the State House Joanna McClinton (Delaware/Philadelphia). State Rep. Ben Waxman (Philadelphia) complemented the Speaker, saying that the legislation is a “comprehensive approach to election reform that tries to dramatically improve the process and makes administration of elections much smoother.”
Among other changes, [https://penncapital-star.com/campaigns-elections/voter-id-and-vote-by-mail-reform-bills-move-to-pa-house] House Bill 1396 would also give election workers up to a week before Election Day to prepare to count mail-in ballots, a process that has been a bottleneck for election results in parts of the state, providing fodder for election deniers.
The vote-by-mail provision has been the subject of numerous lawsuits, including one now before the U.S. Supreme Court, as candidates and parties have argued over how election officials should handle irregularities, such as errors on completed ballots, that are not explicitly addressed.
The bill would make clear that county election officials are required to notify voters if their mail-in ballots have been rejected for the lack of a signature and give the voter an opportunity to “cure” the error.
Upcoming events
As Allegheny County voters prepare for the pivotal 2025 local, municipal and judicial elections, the New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) is hosting two critical public candidate forums to give community members the chance to hear directly from the candidates shaping our schools and courts. These moderated discussions will allow voters to ask questions and learn more about each candidate’s priorities and qualifications before heading to the polls.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/Pittsburgh%20School%20board%20forum%208.5x11.png]
Meet the candidates running for the Pittsburgh School Board and hear how they plan to impact our schools and students.
Moderator: Dr. Kimeka Campbell is our Senior Director of Campaigns and Engagement. She is a recognized community activist, political strategist, and civic engagement leader.
With over 15 years of experience in community organizing, political consulting, and nonprofit leadership, Dr. Campbell has been instrumental in shaping voter engagement strategies and building coalitions for progressive change. She co-founded Young Professionals of Color–Greater Harrisburg (YPOC), an organization dedicated to fostering leadership, advocacy, and professional growth for communities of color.
When: Friday, May 9, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 7:30 PM
Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221
RSVP [https://click.everyaction.com/k/106793662/542679746/-435660973]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/Allegheny%20judicial%20flyer.png]
Get to know the Allegheny County Common Pleas judicial candidates before you cast your ballot.
Your vote shapes our justice system!
Moderator: Rachel Bracken, Alliance for Justice (AFJ), Senior Regional State Courts Manager
Rachel is a Senior Regional State Courts Manager with Alliance for Justice/AFJ Action. In this position, she manages AFJ/AFJ Action’s involvement in state supreme court justice elections and selection processes. Prior to joining the Justice Team, Rachel was on AFJ’s Outreach Team where she helped create AFJ’s State Court Justice Project and launch AFJ Action’s State Courts Matter initiative. She also coordinated AFJ’s networks of law professors, attorneys, and political donors to advocate around federal judicial nominations and federal courts’ impact on our constitutional rights and freedoms.
When: Monday, May 12, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM
Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221
RSVP [https://click.everyaction.com/k/106793661/542679745/229023784]

Request a mail-in ballot or find your local ballot drop-off location here [vote.pa]

We recommend mailing it at least 5 days prior, or delivering it by hand at an official ballot drop off location.
#GoVotePA
SHARE ON INSTAGRAM [https://www.instagram.com/p/DJbyV6DPLdJ/]
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/7237344C447445787878394747342B75584E47375A57552F745265427662334B544A4A36505A58657A5871474B7A5A6E53705571424758325657365969365747/editor_images/WCM%20logo.JPG] #CourtsMatter
This past Friday, Courts delivered wins for Pennsylvania voters [https://www.democracydocket.com/news-alerts/a-double-win-for-voters-in-pennsylvania] . One ruling ensured that undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots will be counted, while the other rejected a bid to purge more than 277,000 from the state’s voter rolls.
First, the 3rd U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals denied [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/445-2025-04-24-Order-denying-PA-Attorney-Generals-motion-to-intervene.pdf] a request by Pennsylvania Attorney General Dave Sunday (R), on behalf of the state, to halt a recent federal court ruling that Pennsylvania must count undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots.
The ruling stemmed from a lawsuit originally filed [https://www.democracydocket.com/cases/pennsylvania-undated-and-wrongly-dated-mail-in-ballots-challenge-eakin/] in 2022, challenging the state’s instructions to county boards of elections that any mail-in ballot that’s timely cast but either missing a date or containing the wrong date on the outer return envelopes should not be counted.
After the federal judge’s ruling in March of this year, the plaintiffs appealed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/441-2025-04-02-Notice-Of-Appeal-.pdf] the decision to the 3rd Circuit. The appeal remains ongoing, but today’s order ensures that counties will count wrongly dated and undated ballots during this month’s primary.
Second, a Pennsylvania district court judge dismissed a lawsuit by the 1789 Foundation to have hundreds of thousands of voters purged from Pennsylvania’s voter rolls.The 1789 Foundation, also known as Citizen_AG, filed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/1-2024-10-29-Complaint.pdf] a lawsuit in October against Pennsylvania Secretary of State Al Schmidt (R) claiming Schmidt failed to maintain voter rolls in line with the National Voter Registration Act.
The group wanted the court to force Pennsylvania to remove 277,000 registrants who did not respond to confirmation notices in the 2020 general election cycle removed from the voter rolls.
District Court judge Robert Mariani dismissed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/67-2025-05-02-Memorandum-.pdf] the 1789 Foundation’s lawsuit on the grounds that the organization lacked standing to bring the case in the first place.
In law, standing [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standing_(law)] is a condition that a party seeking a legal remedy must show they have, by demonstrating to the court, sufficient connection to and harm from the law or action challenged to support the party's participation in the case.
In other news, this year marks the 15th anniversary of a landmark U.S. Supreme Court ruling that fundamentally reshaped the world of campaign finance. For good-government supporters, it’s a somber milestone. Find out why in Spotlight PA’s full report. [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/citizens-united-supreme-court-ruling-campaign-finance-dark-money]
The 2010 decision in Citizens United v. Federal Election Commission , they say, vastly expanded the role of big money in politics and ushered in an era of unprecedented — and in some cases, untraceable — spending on elections by ultra-wealthy special interests.
In Pennsylvania, the court decision’s impact on races for state and local offices was muted — at least early on. That is because of Pennsylvania’s notoriously lax campaign finance laws. Some state lawmakers have sought to tighten rules around so-called “dark money” groups, but their legislation didn’t make it through the full legislature. Continue reading for more on Citizens United and its lasting impact [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/citizens-united-supreme-court-ruling-campaign-finance-dark-money] .
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/DelCo_2.png] We are hiring!
Let's provide civic education together!
We're looking for people passionate about voting rights in Philadelphia, Allegheny, Erie, Lawrence, Bucks, Montgomery, Chester, and Delaware County to help us shape the future of Pennsylvania.
Join our team and help us provide civic education in our communities and register folks to vote!
Positions start at $20 an hour.
Interested? Contact jobs@nppef.org [jobs@nppef.org]
EMAIL US [jobs@nppef.org]
News You Can Use
There are things every one of us can do [https://www.democracydocket.com/opinion/we-have-more-power-than-trump-wants-us-to-believe-heres-what-you-can-do/] in our daily lives to help ensure free and fair elections. Some actions are small — so small that you might dismiss them as unimportant. Don’t. Every important journey begins with a single step, and the first is no less important than the last.
1. Educate Yourself
Elections can be complicated. The rules vary from state to state — and sometimes even from county to county. These rules also change frequently due to new laws, policies or court rulings. My first recommendation: spend time each month learning what’s happening with voting laws in your state and nationally.
Spotlight PA has an 2025 Election Center [https://www.spotlightpa.org/elections] with all the information you need to be prepared to vote this year, including guides to the big races, key dates, and more.
* New: Which Pittsburgh mayoral candidate most aligns with you? Take our quiz [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pittsburgh-mayor-election-2025-candidates-quiz]
* Get answers to your questions with our AI-powered Election Assistant [https://www.spotlightpa.org/elections/assistant/]
* Everything you need to know about mail voting [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-mail-ballot-how-to-request-fill-out-return] , becoming a poll worker [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-how-to-be-poll-worker-guide]
* Everything you need to know about how to vote and where to vote [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pennsylvania-primary-election-day-2025-voter-information-registration]
* Guides to Commonwealth, Superior Courts [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-commonwealth-superior-court-candidates] , vetting judicial candidates [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-candidate-vetting-guide]
* Guides to State College school board [https://www.spotlightpa.org/statecollege/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-state-college-school-board-anderson-huff-barlow-black-desmarais-mcternan-sogor] , Reading City Council president [https://www.spotlightpa.org/berks/2025/04/reading-city-council-president-primary-election-2025]
* Guides to voting as a college student [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-college-student-voting-guide]
En Español
* Guía para votar por correo [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pensilvania-elecciones-primarias-2025-votar-correo-como-llenar] , la presidencia del Concejo Municipal de Reading [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/reading-concejo-municipal-primarias-elecciones-2025]
* Guía para quién está en la papeleta de las primarias [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pensilvania-primarias-elecciones-2025-votar-informacion] , cómo convertirse en trabajador electoral el 20 de mayo [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pensilvania-elecciones-2025-como-convertirse-trabajador-electoral]
2. Stay Engaged
To protect democracy, we all must stay engaged day in and day out. We can’t just show up right before an election and tune out afterward. If we remain committed, democracy will win.
Check out a Montgomery County Commissioner’s effort to educate voters. It's now an emmy-nominated short film. “Running elections in Pennsylvania's third-most-populous county, Neil Makhija said he’s seen the slippery slope from election misinformation to political violence.” ( Philadelphia Inquirer [https://www.inquirer.com/politics/pennsylvania/montgomery-county-commissioner-neil-makhija-emmy-20250508.html] )
3. Share What You Learn
Once you’ve figured out what’s going on, share that information with your networks and community. It might feel awkward to bring up voter suppression or changes in election laws when we’re not in the middle of a major election — but local elections happen in off years, and special elections are more common than you think. Now is the perfect time to start conversations about voting.
Here, in Pennsylvania, we vote every year, twice a year!
4. Join or Support Pro-Democracy Organizations
Is partisan politics not for you? There are numerous nonprofits and grassroots organizations working every day to protect voting rights and ensure free and fair elections. Supporting these groups with your time or resources helps these groups do their work.
Check out the work New PA Project has been up to lately below. AND , check out our upcoming events listed above!
5. Become a Trained Poll Worker
Many counties face a shortage of poll workers, especially during election season. Being a poll worker requires dedication, attention to detail and a willingness to be trained. It’s a meaningful way to serve your community and help protect the integrity of the voting process.
Here are some resources that may be helpful:
Pennsylvania Capital-Star - Advocacy groups offer training for Pa. primary poll watchers [https://penncapital-star.com/briefs/advocacy-groups-offer-training-for-pa-primary-poll-watchers/]
Spotlight PA - Election 2025: How to become a poll worker for the PA primary [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-how-to-be-poll-worker-guide/]
Franklin County Free Press - Poll Workers Needed for Pennsylvania’s 2025 Primary Election [https://fcfreepresspa.com/poll-workers-needed-for-pennsylvanias-2025-primary-election/]
6. Engage Your Elected Officials
Call and write your elected officials at the federal, state and local levels. Tell them that protecting voting rights and ensuring free and fair elections are your top priorities. Even better, show up at public meetings. Ask them directly what they’re doing to combat voter suppression. Be informed about pending legislation and urge them to support pro-democracy initiatives. This applies no matter where you live or who represents you — Democrat or Republican. Constituent pressure always matters. Don’t let anyone tell you otherwise.
7. Vote in Every Election
Yes — every election. That means local elections, primary elections and special elections. Become a super-voter. The more you vote, the more informed and engaged you become. You’ll also be better positioned to notice and report problems — like changes to polling locations or voting equipment — that could impact turnout or accessibility.
Make a plan to vote in the primary election, Tuesday, May 20th. When are you voting this year? Who are you going with? How are you voting this year -- by mail or on Election Day? As a reminder, the last day to request a mail ballot is Tuesday, May 13th!
Recent Events

We amplified voices that have been silenced, advocated against injustice, and showed that solidarity is not just a word—it is a force that creates change.
This event was a declaration that Pennsylvania stands against injustice and division.
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/61467144756E73314C4E7335774562454C6644444649732F492B6843473664593068482F7644555678714E564D50363235594F7336424671626839454D43762F/editor_images/signal-2025-05-05-120402.jpeg] [https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/61467144756E73314C4E7335774562454C6644444649732F492B6843473664593068482F7644555678714E564D50363235594F7336424671626839454D43762F/editor_images/signal-2025-05-05-122847.jpeg]

NPPEF on the Ground
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/356574676F576F4656484552317542704442666A614C4C4D5334613152463762514A70726B39646D4F494334727651544F3756582B436D446461684F5A536E4F/editor_images/unnamed%20%281%29.jpg] Last Monday, May 5th was the last day to register to vote for the primary election!
Our teams were out in their communities leading up to the voter registration deadline on Monday. We helped folks ensure they have the ability to exercise their right to vote in the primary on Tuesday, May 20th.
Our teams are currently getting ready for GOTV - a time of year where we are on the ground connecting with our communities and mobilizing them to vote.

An omnibus bill is a legislative bill that combines multiple, sometimes unrelated, provisions or bills into one single bill. Moderator: Dr. Kimeka Campbell is our Senior Director of Campaigns and Engagement. She is a recognized community activist, political strategist, and civic engagement leader. With over 15 years of experience in community organizing, political consulting, and nonprofit leadership, Dr. Campbell has been instrumental in shaping voter engagement strategies and building coalitions for progressive change. She co-founded Young Professionals of Color–Greater Harrisburg (YPOC), an organization dedicated to fostering leadership, advocacy, and professional growth for communities of color. When: Friday, May 9, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 7:30 PM Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221 RSVP [] [] Get to know the Allegheny County Common Pleas judicial candidates before you cast your ballot. Your vote shapes our justice system! Moderator: Rachel Bracken, Alliance for Justice (AFJ), Senior Regional State Courts Manager Rachel is a Senior Regional State Courts Manager with Alliance for Justice/AFJ Action. In this position, she manages AFJ/AFJ Action’s involvement in state supreme court justice elections and selection processes. Prior to joining the Justice Team, Rachel was on AFJ’s Outreach Team where she helped create AFJ’s State Court Justice Project and launch AFJ Action’s State Courts Matter initiative. She also coordinated AFJ’s networks of law professors, attorneys, and political donors to advocate around federal judicial nominations and federal courts’ impact on our constitutional rights and freedoms. When: Monday, May 12, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221 RSVP [] Social Media of the Week [] The deadline to request your mail-in ballot for the primary election is May 13th at 5PM. Request a mail-in ballot or find your local ballot drop-off location here [vote.pa] Your county election office must receive your completed mail ballot by 8PM on May 20th. We recommend mailing it at least 5 days prior, or delivering it by hand at an official ballot drop off location. #GoVotePA SHARE ON INSTAGRAM [] [] #CourtsMatter This past Friday, Courts delivered wins for Pennsylvania voters [] . One ruling ensured that undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots will be counted, while the other rejected a bid to purge more than 277,000 from the state’s voter rolls. First, the 3rd U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals denied [] a request by Pennsylvania Attorney General Dave Sunday (R), on behalf of the state, to halt a recent federal court ruling that Pennsylvania must count undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots. The ruling stemmed from a lawsuit originally filed [] in 2022, challenging the state’s instructions to county boards of elections that any mail-in ballot that’s timely cast but either missing a date or containing the wrong date on the outer return envelopes should not be counted. After the federal judge’s ruling in March of this year, the plaintiffs appealed [] the decision to the 3rd Circuit. The appeal remains ongoing, but today’s order ensures that counties will count wrongly dated and undated ballots during this month’s primary. Second, a Pennsylvania district court judge dismissed a lawsuit by the 1789 Foundation to have hundreds of thousands of voters purged from Pennsylvania’s voter rolls.The 1789 Foundation, also known as CitizenAG, filed [] a lawsuit in October against Pennsylvania Secretary of State Al Schmidt (R) claiming Schmidt failed to maintain voter rolls in line with the National Voter Registration Act. The group wanted the court to force Pennsylvania to remove 277,000 registrants who did not respond to confirmation notices in the 2020 general election cycle removed from the voter rolls. District Court judge Robert Mariani dismissed [] the 1789 Foundation’s lawsuit on the grounds that the organization lacked standing to bring the case in the first place. In law, standing [)] is a condition that a party seeking a legal remedy must show they have, by demonstrating to the court, sufficient connection to and harm from the law or action challenged to support the party's participation in the case. In other news, this year marks the 15th anniversary of a landmark U.S. Supreme Court ruling that fundamentally reshaped the world of campaign finance. For good-government supporters, it’s a somber milestone. Find out why in Spotlight PA’s full report. [] The 2010 decision in Citizens United v. Federal Election Commission , they say, vastly expanded the role of big money in politics and ushered in an era of unprecedented — and in some cases, untraceable — spending on elections by ultra-wealthy special interests. In Pennsylvania, the court decision’s impact on races for state and local offices was muted — at least early on. That is because of Pennsylvania’s notoriously lax campaign finance laws. Some state lawmakers have sought to tighten rules around so-called “dark money” groups, but their legislation didn’t make it through the full legislature. Continue reading for more on Citizens United and its lasting impact [] . [] We are hiring! Let's provide civic education together! We're looking for people passionate about voting rights in Philadelphia, Allegheny, Erie, Lawrence, Bucks, Montgomery, Chester, and Delaware County to help us shape the future of Pennsylvania. Join our team and help us provide civic education in our communities and register folks to vote! Positions start at $20 an hour. Interested? Contact jobs@nppef.org [jobs@nppef.org] EMAIL US [jobs@nppef.org] News You Can Use There are things every one of us can do [] in our daily lives to help ensure free and fair elections. Some actions are small — so small that you might dismiss them as unimportant. Don’t. Every important journey begins with a single step, and the first is no less important than the last. 1. Educate Yourself Elections can be complicated. The rules vary from state to state — and sometimes even from county to county. These rules also change frequently due to new laws, policies or court rulings. My first recommendation: spend time each month learning what’s happening with voting laws in your state and nationally. Spotlight PA has an 2025 Election Center [] with all the information you need to be prepared to vote this year, including guides to the big races, key dates, and more. New: Which Pittsburgh mayoral candidate most aligns with you? Take our quiz [] Get answers to your questions with our AI-powered Election Assistant [] Everything you need to know about mail voting [] , becoming a poll worker [] Everything you need to know about how to vote and where to vote [] Guides to Commonwealth, Superior Courts [] , vetting judicial candidates [] Guides to State College school board [] , Reading City Council president [] Guides to voting as a college student [] En Español Guía para votar por correo [] , la presidencia del Concejo Municipal de Reading [] * Guía para quién está en la papeleta de las primarias [] , cómo convertirse en trabajador electoral el 20 de mayo [] 2. Stay Engaged To protect democracy, we all must stay engaged day in and day out. We can’t just show up right before an election and tune out afterward. If we remain committed, democracy will win. Check out a Montgomery County Commissioner’s effort to educate voters. It's now an emmy-nominated short film. “Running elections in Pennsylvania's third-most-populous county, Neil Makhija said he’s seen the slippery slope from election misinformation to political violence.” ( Philadelphia Inquirer [] ) 3. Share What You Learn Once you’ve figured out what’s going on, share that information with your networks and community. It might feel awkward to bring up voter suppression or changes in election laws when we’re not in the middle of a major election — but local elections happen in off years, and special elections are more common than you think. Now is the perfect time to start conversations about voting. Here, in Pennsylvania, we vote every year, twice a year! 4. Join or Support Pro-Democracy Organizations Is partisan politics not for you? There are numerous nonprofits and grassroots organizations working every day to protect voting rights and ensure free and fair elections. Supporting these groups with your time or resources helps these groups do their work. Check out the work New PA Project has been up to lately below. AND , check out our upcoming events listed above! 5. Become a Trained Poll Worker Many counties face a shortage of poll workers, especially during election season. Being a poll worker requires dedication, attention to detail and a willingness to be trained. It’s a meaningful way to serve your community and help protect the integrity of the voting process. Here are some resources that may be helpful: Pennsylvania Capital-Star - Advocacy groups offer training for Pa. primary poll watchers [] Spotlight PA - Election 2025: How to become a poll worker for the PA primary [] Franklin County Free Press - Poll Workers Needed for Pennsylvania’s 2025 Primary Election [] 6. Engage Your Elected Officials Call and write your elected officials at the federal, state and local levels. Tell them that protecting voting rights and ensuring free and fair elections are your top priorities. Even better, show up at public meetings. Ask them directly what they’re doing to combat voter suppression. Be informed about pending legislation and urge them to support pro-democracy initiatives. This applies no matter where you live or who represents you — Democrat or Republican. Constituent pressure always matters. Don’t let anyone tell you otherwise. 7. Vote in Every Election Yes — every election. That means local elections, primary elections and special elections. Become a super-voter. The more you vote, the more informed and engaged you become. You’ll also be better positioned to notice and report problems — like changes to polling locations or voting equipment — that could impact turnout or accessibility. Make a plan to vote in the primary election, Tuesday, May 20th. When are you voting this year? Who are you going with? How are you voting this year -- by mail or on Election Day? As a reminder, the last day to request a mail ballot is Tuesday, May 13th! Recent Events [] Solidarity for Immigrants On Monday, May 5th, also Cinco de Mayo, over 300 people, representing 18 community organizations from across Pennsylvania, gathered at the State Capitol in Harrisburg to rally for immigrant rights. We amplified voices that have been silenced, advocated against injustice, and showed that solidarity is not just a word—it is a force that creates change. This event was a declaration that Pennsylvania stands against injustice and division. [] [] [] NPPEF on the Ground [] Last Monday, May 5th was the last day to register to vote for the primary election! Our teams were out in their communities leading up to the voter registration deadline on Monday. We helped folks ensure they have the ability to exercise their right to vote in the primary on Tuesday, May 20th. Our teams are currently getting ready for GOTV - a time of year where we are on the ground connecting with our communities and mobilizing them to vote. It's our favorite time of year to build relationships with those we hope to uplift. Dauphin County [] [] Delaware County [] [] Luzerne County [] [] Super voters vote twice a year, every year ! Thank you for doing your part to stay informed and engaged! [] Last day to register to request a mail ballot: Tuesday, May 13th Primary Election: Tuesday, May 20th Last day to register to vote for the General Election: Monday, October 20th Last day to request mail-in ballot: Tuesday, October 28th General Election: Tuesday, November 4th Important Links: Voter Hotlines: PA Department of State Website [vote.pa.gov] Voter Protection Hotline: 1-866-OUR-VOTE Upcoming elections [] DOS PA Voter Hotline: 1-877-VOTESPA Mail-in and Absentee Ballot [] Spanish/English Hotline: 1-866-VE-Y-VOTA Fact-Checking Election Claims [] Asian Languages Hotline: 1-888-API-VOTE Arabic/English Hotline: 1-844-YALLA-US Paid for by the New PA Project Education Fund New PA Project Education Fund PO Box 453 West Chester, PA 19381 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt **[went off at NBC reporter Yamiche Alcindor](https://substack.com/redirect/8253c8e8-525d-4e4f-89e1-8affe2b03a33?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** for saying President Donald Trump made false claims about the persecution of white South African farmers during his meeting with President Cyril Ramaphosa earlier this week.
_This was brutal ..._
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/8253c8e8-525d-4e4f-89e1-8affe2b03a33?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/8253c8e8-525d-4e4f-89e1-8affe2b03a33?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"The President showed a video that he said showed more than 1,000 burial sites of white South Africans that he said were murdered. We know that that was not true and the video wasn't showing that," Alcindor told Leavitt.
**["What's not true Yamiche?!" Leavitt demanded.](https://substack.com/redirect/8253c8e8-525d-4e4f-89e1-8affe2b03a33?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
Alcindor said it was not true that the video showed "a burial site" and that the president's claim was "unsubstantiated.
"No, it is true that that video showed crosses in south Africa of white farmers who have been killed and persecuted because of the color of their skin, and those crosses are representing their lives and the fact that they are now dead and their government did nothing about it!" Leavitt fired back.
Alcindor pushed back, again calling the president's claims "unsubstantiated."
Leavitt cited the Associated Press for having previously reported on the white crosses monument in South Africa, "The caption from the Associated Press is 'each cross marks a white farmer who has been killed in a farm murder!'"
"So it is substantiated, not just by that video, and the physical evidence,... but also by another outlet in this room, the Associated Press, so you should take it up with them!" Leavitt added. "That's a ridiculous line of questioning!"
**[Watch the press secretary put the mainstream media in their place:](https://substack.com/redirect/8253c8e8-525d-4e4f-89e1-8affe2b03a33?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[ Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/8253c8e8-525d-4e4f-89e1-8affe2b03a33?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

# Kevin Kiley fact checks Jasmine Crockett in front of the nation for illegal immigrant "due process" argument
Congressman Kevin Kiley **[clashed with Democrat Rep. Jasmine Crockett](https://substack.com/redirect/03638aea-3822-4289-a778-54222193c4ef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** during a hearing debate about illegal immigrations right to due process.
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/03638aea-3822-4289-a778-54222193c4ef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/03638aea-3822-4289-a778-54222193c4ef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Texas Congresswoman Jasmine Crockett accused Republicans of ignoring the Constitution based on the recent increase of ICE raids ordered by the Trump administration, catching and deporting some of the millions of illegal immigrants that **[came to the U.S. under the Biden Administration's border disorder.](https://substack.com/redirect/03638aea-3822-4289-a778-54222193c4ef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
"Right now, it seems like y'all don't care about The Constitution, unless it's the second amendment, and that's misinterpreted as well." Crockett said.
Kiley asked Crockett a great question, "Do you believe that someone who has snuck into our country, entered the United States illegally - that the due process requirements for that individual are the same as they are for that of a US citizen?"
Crockett replied, "Per The Constitution, if you are on our soil, you are guaranteed due process. And the fact that there's even a question, is why we are struggling right now," going on to say that Trump even signed a document that rejected birthright citizenship.
"The Supreme Court has made it very clear that due process is a context dependent inquiry and someone who is here illegally in this country facing deportation, the requirements of due process are specific to that context. The problem is that during the last administration, **[we had 10 million people who came into this country without the least bit of vetting,](https://substack.com/redirect/03638aea-3822-4289-a778-54222193c4ef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **and it's created an enormous backlog in our immigration courts," Kiley said, laying down the hammer.
Kiley then exposed the Democrats for denying approval to amplify immigration resources.
"I think it's highly problematic to hear arguments about due process from the side that is actually voting against providing the resources that are needed for due process, but I think it's even more problematic to make the argument that an illegal immigrant, perhaps with a criminal record facing deportation, should receive the full measure of due process that an American citizen does."
Republican Rep. Jim Jordan stepped in, quoting former Justice of the Peace, William Rehnquist: "He firmly and repeatedly endorsed the proposition that Congress may make rules as to aliens that would be unacceptable if applied to citizens."
**[Watch the heated showdown:](https://substack.com/redirect/03638aea-3822-4289-a778-54222193c4ef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[ Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/03638aea-3822-4289-a778-54222193c4ef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

**➤➤ BREAKING:**** Secretary of State Rubio dismisses judicial authority in Senate testimony** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108253449/547349590/-209663064?refcode=em-5.23.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNTIzMTFlMC0xNjM4LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NOIjyV42qR4h7q6qdSH15loJo7qwqo-409JjqTQ6b50=) They’re not trying to hide it anymore, friend. Secretary of State Marco Rubio just made a startling declaration: **neither he nor the president is bound by judicial oversight in conducting foreign policy.** Clearly he and Donald Trump have no concern for checks and balances. When questioned about the deportation of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a Maryland resident mistakenly removed to El Salvador, Rubio spewed unverified claims about Garcia's alleged criminal affiliations, and even said, **_"No judge can tell me or the president how to conduct foreign policy."_** Horrifying. Secretary Rubio and the Trump Administration want to undermine the separation of powers and threaten the very fabric of our democracy. If no one’s watching the executive branch, there’s no telling what abuses they’ll get away with – or how far they’ll go to put their power above the law. The Democratic Association of Secretaries of State (DASS) recognizes the importance of upholding the rule of law at every level of government. _Unlike the current administration’s Secretary of State,_ our Secretaries of State are dedicated to upholding the integrity of our democratic institutions. [**Your support is crucial. name: Indiana GOP
subject: Celebration and Anticipation
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE INDIANA REPUBLICAN STATE COMMITTEE
body:
| | |
---
| |
---
| | _**Celebration and Anticipation**_
---
Friends,

This has been a week of back-to-back conservative victories, both in DC and at home. Indiana’s Republican delegation ran on President Trump’s America First agenda, and yesterday, they delivered. The passage of the One, Big, Beautiful Bill marks a historic moment: moving forward the largest tax cut in American history and decisive action to secure our southern border. This is what Republican leadership looks like: keeping promises, defending American workers, and setting the stage for a Trump economic boom that will not only match but exceed the success of his first term. Senator Jim Banks joined President Trump to announce the Golden Dome, which will not only secure our nation's safety for generations to come, but provide well paying jobs to Hoosiers across our state. Just today, Governor Braun announced that the USDA signed his waiver to remove sugary drinks and candy from the taxpayer funded SNAP program, setting us on the path to Make Indiana Healthy Again! I join countless Hoosiers in anticipation of the Indy 500, as more than 1 in 1000 Americans and spectators from across the world arrive in our capital. As the daughter of a former race car driver, I wait all year for the greatest spectacle in racing, and as your Chairwoman, I couldn't imagine a better way to showcase all that our state has to offer. As always, thank you for being a part of our team. Please feel free to reach out if there's anything I can assist with.
---
| |
---
| | Lana Keesling, Indiana GOP Chairwoman
---
|
---
|
---
| |

| | _**Governor Braun Speaks at the 500 Festival Memorial Service**_
---
---
Today, Governor Braun joined service members and veterans from across the state to honor the brave Hoosiers killed in the line of duty on the steps of the Indianapolis War Memorial.
---
_**Governor Braun Announces USDA Waiver to Remove Sugary Drinks and Candy from SNAP Program**_
---

Today, Governor Braun joined service members and veterans from across the state to honor the brave Hoosiers killed in the line of duty on the steps of the Indianapolis War Memorial. **What's he saying:** "Indiana is proud to lead the way in the Make America Healthy Again agenda by making this common sense move to return the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program to its intended purpose: nutrition." \- Governor Braun via [X](http://links.email.in-gop.com/s/c/zaaGpmzymjyMLEvTXqM6COF9eeXnqTQTl-UP_XkF4naCmQjIaO7JDrmUuRmooF1sUTYNKOsmH_WsPyUmIrWg-759f0P_PYHTinv7G7aH1JtsFO5QgK8m62oBzAmANMbK1oqveMtp7OeRoRzm8Bh9t8vzm3LuqiDN1k6I152efeWvzm62OUIV_0gp_Lx1IR4jA1pWX7Y_RjbY5TEWpQTCYJLS40bpsEEJadNyd4SdodnrLOV6kW5_aMVEauPX01b-_EnGBfMD_gCo-cm4VhcQB1MxpqSwUneNCl8-Hw/fiM5YcP21FhjxpUOGaJ6BM4wWQ704v9h/9).

**Governor Mike Braun** clean_body: | | | | | | | Celebration and Anticipation Friends, This has been a week of back-to-back conservative victories, both in DC and at home. Indiana’s Republican delegation ran on President Trump’s America First agenda, and yesterday, they delivered. The passage of the One, Big, Beautiful Bill marks a historic moment: moving forward the largest tax cut in American history and decisive action to secure our southern border. This is what Republican leadership looks like: keeping promises, defending American workers, and setting the stage for a Trump economic boom that will not only match but exceed the success of his first term. Senator Jim Banks joined President Trump to announce the Golden Dome, which will not only secure our nation's safety for generations to come, but provide well paying jobs to Hoosiers across our state. Just today, Governor Braun announced that the USDA signed his waiver to remove sugary drinks and candy from the taxpayer funded SNAP program, setting us on the path to Make Indiana Healthy Again! I join countless Hoosiers in anticipation of the Indy 500, as more than 1 in 1000 Americans and spectators from across the world arrive in our capital. As the daughter of a former race car driver, I wait all year for the greatest spectacle in racing, and as your Chairwoman, I couldn't imagine a better way to showcase all that our state has to offer. As always, thank you for being a part of our team. Please feel free to reach out if there's anything I can assist with. | | | | Lana Keesling, Indiana GOP Chairwoman | | | | | | Governor Braun Speaks at the 500 Festival Memorial Service Today, Governor Braun joined service members and veterans from across the state to honor the brave Hoosiers killed in the line of duty on the steps of the Indianapolis War Memorial. Governor Braun Announces USDA Waiver to Remove Sugary Drinks and Candy from SNAP Program Today, Governor Braun joined service members and veterans from across the state to honor the brave Hoosiers killed in the line of duty on the steps of the Indianapolis War Memorial. What's he saying: "Indiana is proud to lead the way in the Make America Healthy Again agenda by making this common sense move to return the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program to its intended purpose: nutrition." \- Governor Braun via . | White County Benton County Putnam County IRFW Chairwoman's County Visits: 36 Indiana GOP is Hiring Interns for the Fall Semester Looking to jumpstart your career in politics, public relations, or grassroots organizing? The Indiana Republican Party is now accepting applications for our Summer and Fall Internship Program! ✅ Gain hands-on experience in campaign strategy, communications, and event planning ✅ Work alongside state leaders and build valuable connections ✅ Make a real impact on Republican efforts in Indiana Apply Today! Spots are limited! 🔗 | 📱 News You Can Use Governor Mike Braun Republican Governor's in signing a letter of support for the passage of President Trump's One Big Beautiful Bill. Lieutenant Governor Micah Beckwith will as the keynote speaker at the Right to Life Dinner in Indianapolis, reaffirming his administration's commitment to the sanctity of life and the protection of the unborn. Secretary Diego Morales that Simon Property Group, one of the nation's largest and most prominent real estate companies, has officially transferred its corporate registration from the state of Delaware to Indiana. Treasurer Daniel Elliott recently the 77th anniversary of Israel's Independence. He wrote, "Indiana’s investments in Israel are not only good for Hoosiers, but a strong moral statement about standing with our friends in the face of terror." Comptroller Elise Nieshalla to the leadership of the Indiana Courts a report on our State's finances, providing information on Indiana's solid fiscal position. Attorney General Todd Rokita a 29-count administrative complaint against the real estate broker license of an Avon Resident. “We want to root out this kind of fraudulent misconduct wherever it occurs, and we want to hold perpetrators accountable,” Attorney General Rokita said. “It’s all part of our mission of protecting Hoosiers and upholding the rule of law.” Rokita thanked his team for their work on this case. Senator Todd Young remarks on the Senate floor about the importance of remembering those who paid the ultimate price for our country, ahead of Memorial Day. Senator Jim Banks President Trump in the Oval Office for the announcement of the "Golden Dome" and delivered remarks on how the program and President Trump's tariffs are benefiting Hoosiers. Congressman Rudy Yakym the Safeguarding Essential Cables through Undersea Risk Elimination (SECURE) American Telecommunications Act. Yakym aims to ensure that we are able to hold adversaries like China and Russia accountable for cutting submarine telecommunications cables. Congressman Marlin Stutzman to pass the One Big Beautiful Bill Act. " The One Big Beautiful Bill Act is the start of America’s return to fiscal sanity,” said Rep. Stutzman. “This bill is the single best piece of legislation I have voted for when it comes to fixing our country’s fiscal future while serving in Washington, D.C." Congressman Jim Baird the passage of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act. He wrote, “I was proud to vote for the One Big, Beautiful Bill. It is a win for Hoosiers and the American people and codifies President Trump’s successful agenda. This big, beautiful bill delivers historic tax relief for the American people, including seniors, eliminates taxes on tips and overtime, makes strategic investments in our military, secures our borders, authorizes the completion of the border wall, and invests in our farmers and producers." Congresswoman Victoria Spartz the Defender of Economic Freedom Award from Club for Growth and the Award for Conservative Excellence from CPAC. Congressman Jefferson Shreve National Police Week and thanked "the brave men and women in law enforcement." Congressman Mark Messmer a statement regarding the passage of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act , "House Republicans worked hard to deliver President Trump’s Big Beautiful Bill. This comprehensive legislation increases funding for border security, protects the needs of our nation’s farmers, and provides necessary tax relief for Hoosiers and all American.s” Congresswoman Erin Houchin a statement regarding the passage of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act , "The U.S. House of Representatives passed the One Big, Beautiful Bill this morning—delivering on President Trump’s America First agenda. This is a bill for hardworking American families. No tax on tips, no tax on overtime, and tax relief for seniors on Social Security. body:
Here’s your 60-second download on what’s happening in the Trump administration and the GOP-led Congress this week: |
---
**House passes Trump's deficit-swelling tax bill, with big Medicaid changes**
Yesterday, House Republicans passed their dangerous tax bill. It proposes **extreme cuts to Medicaid, Medicare and SNAP food benefits** that low-income, disabled and elderly folks rely on to survive — while providing major tax breaks to the wealthiest Americans. If this bill becomes law, more than 7 million Americans will lose access to essential health care through Medicaid.
* * *
---
**Deadly storms highlight concern about Trump's cuts to National Weather Service**
The National Weather Service is a vital part of our national response to major weather disasters. Cuts at NWS, FEMA and other government entities hurt the people who dedicate their lives to help struggling communities prevent, prepare and recover from these disasters. With a string of deadly storms hitting communities across the U.S.,**the Trump administration and DOGE’s attempts to cut funding and reduce staff will have devastating impacts.**
* * *
---
**Trump takes on Walmart over tariff price hikes, setting up potential showdown with retail industry**
Trump’s trade wars and tariff hikes are set to drive up prices at popular retailers like Walmart and Target. This will**impact hardworking American families and reverberate through the entire economy —** causing uncertainty, impacting Americans’ pocketbooks and hurting small businesses.
* * *
These updates aren’t meant to fear-monger. It’s important to stay informed so we know how to push back and **get off the sidelines.**

name: Deb Haaland
subject: A broken leg and 3 options
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gabe Vasquez for Congress
body:
A few years ago while visiting Shenandoah National Park, I took a bit of a tumble.

Gabe Vasquez won the most competitive Democratic seat in Congress in 2022. He's running to be a champion for working families and build an economy that benefits everyone. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/lNYIdkhPikOBrh6RGc-KWg2?unsubscribedata=oYyCwuPp0eCthbSTH3MSZJxXoS8%2Bh8sUE1P6dilnu1XQHX9zkhK3mW02MQeXlQYVqNFM2X7lWYM15p9ZTbHeGCbGI7bHS2YMJDXwYjnOBvwK1qTebaDyv7tRjDb3oFIp3M%2BrLWYLqvkW5W7c0uG2CWq65LJXoY4hbUx5HrMjIhkpA6W692nBC%2FDRdCDv1gmkXEfRRmUcrQusJmIrKc4jPyguGWo6LJOk7ITCwTtipowGSkKHTED7peWF%2BMRuCB7e75yRMZ4zb9XmlsTJdi779MMyV%2FGo8nC2DRTvNx0c61ymhUNjFFMlf%2B4x8O6oJxW7jFJXQ001%2FWT9NYMv46W8pVFoVqqNXjt1e7RQZqctsGIxbYwp6GoKFt1FiAANGMTWbO%2BHO1n%2FxISso2Nu3zP70xv3SxMbLiGZCpXb9pAQ%2Fvg%3D)
---
| | Deb Haaland here. A few years ago while visiting Shenandoah National Park, I took _a bit_ of a tumble. I was hiking up the side of a mountain when I slipped on a loose rock. It was a total freak accident — I couldn’t believe it! But there I was, covered in dirt with a pain in my leg and ankle and three options:
* Get airlifted down in a helicopter,
* take eight National Park staff away from their jobs to carry me down,
* or hike down myself.

**I chose to walk back down the mountain,** because I couldn’t stomach the idea of taking our National Park staff away from their work, even for a few hours. It turns out I hiked down all that way with a broken leg. I’ll be honest, I didn’t think my leg was actually broken at the time. The federal workers who care for our public lands have absolutely crucial jobs to do. They preserve and protect our public lands for millions of visitors each year and for future generations. **Donald Trump and Elon Musk have made it clear they intend to auction off our lands to extractive industries like Big Oil. They’ve attempted to fire our dedicated National Parks and National Forests workers en masse.** As Governor of New Mexico, I will be a fierce defender of our rivers, mesas, farmlands, and forests. I will always stand up for the dedicated workers who serve our communities. [If you’re with me, will you split a donation to support my campaign and Gabe Vasquez?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108254646/547353884/-173865004?amount=10&source=SBD_GV_EM_FR_052325_HAALAND_60DO_D2_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzAxMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZDYxNjNkMy0xOTM4LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=REsVoMQs8LHxZ-tCaGENXj8nx5gOufzPmKamKdMDDQo=) | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately and be split evenly between Deb Haaland and Gabe Vasquez.

As a former Secretary of the Interior, it breaks my heart to see what this administration is doing to exploit our lands and those who care for them.
**But just like the day I chose to hike down the mountain on my broken leg, I am determined to stand up and do right by the workers who proudly serve our nation.**
[New Mexicans need a governor who isn’t afraid of the tough fights that lie ahead. I can’t win this campaign without you, so please split a donation of $10 if you can afford it today.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108254653/547353891/-173865004?amount=10&source=SBD_GV_EM_FR_052325_HAALAND_60DO_D2_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzAxMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZDYxNjNkMy0xOTM4LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=REsVoMQs8LHxZ-tCaGENXj8nx5gOufzPmKamKdMDDQo=)
Thanks for reading. It means a lot that Gabe allowed me to share this moment with you — because the fight to protect our public lands belongs to all of us.
Be fierce,
Deb
P.S. In case you’re wondering, not even a broken left fibula could keep me away from our beautiful parks for long. Here’s a photo of me from one of my favorite places to visit, Monument Valley Navajo Tribal Park.

clean_body: A few years ago while visiting Shenandoah National Park, I took a bit of a tumble. Gabe Vasquez won the most competitive Democratic seat in Congress in 2022. He's running to be a champion for working families and build an economy that benefits everyone. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | Deb Haaland here. A few years ago while visiting Shenandoah National Park, I took a bit of a tumble. I was hiking up the side of a mountain when I slipped on a loose rock. It was a total freak accident — I couldn’t believe it! But there I was, covered in dirt with a pain in my leg and ankle and three options: Get airlifted down in a helicopter, take eight National Park staff away from their jobs to carry me down, or hike down myself. I chose to walk back down the mountain, because I couldn’t stomach the idea of taking our National Park staff away from their work, even for a few hours. It turns out I hiked down all that way with a broken leg. I’ll be honest, I didn’t think my leg was actually broken at the time. The federal workers who care for our public lands have absolutely crucial jobs to do. They preserve and protect our public lands for millions of visitors each year and for future generations. Donald Trump and Elon Musk have made it clear they intend to auction off our lands to extractive industries like Big Oil. They’ve attempted to fire our dedicated National Parks and National Forests workers en masse. As Governor of New Mexico, I will be a fierce defender of our rivers, mesas, farmlands, and forests. I will always stand up for the dedicated workers who serve our communities. = | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately and be split evenly between Deb Haaland and Gabe Vasquez. | = | = | = | = | = | = As a former Secretary of the Interior, it breaks my heart to see what this administration is doing to exploit our lands and those who care for them. But just like the day I chose to hike down the mountain on my broken leg, I am determined to stand up and do right by the workers who proudly serve our nation. = Thanks for reading. It means a lot that Gabe allowed me to share this moment with you — because the fight to protect our public lands belongs to all of us. Be fierce, Deb P.S. In case you’re wondering, not even a broken left fibula could keep me away from our beautiful parks for long. Here’s a photo of me from one of my favorite places to visit, Monument Valley Navajo Tribal Park. = | | | ---|---|--- = Together, we flipped NM-02 in 2022. Now, Gabe is busy in Congress fighting for reproductive choice, economic opportunity, affordable healthcare, and the best interests of the people. Gabe won the most competitive Democratic seat in Congress in 2022, and is running to be a champion for working families and build an economy that benefits everyone. | = = Paid for by Gabe Vasquez for Congress Gabe Vasquez for Congress Drawer L Mesilla, NM 88046 United States If you would like to unsubscribe, click here: To receive less email from Gabe Vasquez for Congress, . body:
Our kids deserve better. Full stop. [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108254683/547354008/693975070?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNTFjNWJmNy0xOTM4LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cCyKAjDCjhjiEq5paoMaj2TKvvG5gNDfUF2ft3N1YcE=&emci=c318bb33-ed37-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=351c5bf7-1938-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=15820338)
---

Peter,

As a pediatrician, Kim has spent her entire career using fact-based, practical solutions to care for her patients. And as a medical professional, Kim has a duty to prioritize the health of our children based on reality— _not conspiracy theories._

But now, Trump-appointed Health and Human Services Secretary and conspiracy theorist Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is rolling out his “Make America Healthy Again” (MAHA) report. **And it’s as dangerous as it sounds.**

The report blames “overprescribing” medication for childhood chronic illnesses, lumping in life-saving vaccines and mental health medication for children as bad practices. He’s calling for a full study to review childhood vaccination timelines–something that would completely upend our already safe and effective schedule of vaccines for kids.

This is the same anti-vaccine nonsense that RFK Jr. has been peddling for years. As a medical professional, Kim isn't going to be silent as RFK pushes pseudoscience over fact-based medical decisions.

Vaccines save lives, full stop. Yet because more and more parents have already fallen victim to the GOP’s fear-mongering tactics, we're seeing once eradicated diseases like measles, mumps, whooping cough, and even polio reemerge with devastating consequences.

**[We cannot allow RFK’s policies to go into effect. Chip in $5, or whatever you can, to help Kim keep standing up for science, truth, and our kids’ health.>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108254684/547354009/-1351093942?refcode=em25_250523_MAHA_fndd_gq_act___dkscom_ettx&&&&utm_campaign=em25_250523_MAHA_fndd_gq_act___dkscom_ettx&&&&utm_medium=email&&&&refcode2=351c5bf7-1938-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&refcode2=351c5bf7-1938-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNTFjNWJmNy0xOTM4LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cCyKAjDCjhjiEq5paoMaj2TKvvG5gNDfUF2ft3N1YcE=)**

It gets worse, Peter: When asked directly in a hearing whether people should take his medical advice, Kennedy admitted:
_**“I don't think people should be taking medical advice from me.”**_
But the problem is—as Health Secretary, **people ARE listening to his advice...**

And right now, his position is to give unscientific ideas a government seal of approval. This isn't just reckless, it's beyond dangerous.

**RFK Jr. is NOT fit for this job** , just like Trump isn’t qualified to be appointing his extremist allies and conspiracy theorists to high-ranking government positions. More than ever before, we need leaders who will stand up for science and protect our families—not those who push pseudoscience and conspiracy theories onto our children.

**[Kim is fighting every single day in Congress to be the leader that we deserve—but she needs you with her in order to strengthen this campaign and protect this seat as we look ahead to 2026. If you can, please consider chipping in now if you stand with her.>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108254685/547354010/-1351093942?refcode=em25_250523_MAHA_fndd_gq_act___dkscom_ettx&&&&utm_campaign=em25_250523_MAHA_fndd_gq_act___dkscom_ettx&&&&utm_medium=email&&&&refcode2=351c5bf7-1938-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&refcode2=351c5bf7-1938-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNTFjNWJmNy0xOTM4LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=cCyKAjDCjhjiEq5paoMaj2TKvvG5gNDfUF2ft3N1YcE=)**

clean_body: Our kids deserve better. Full stop. Peter, As a pediatrician, Kim has spent her entire career using fact-based, practical solutions to care for her patients. And as a medical professional, Kim has a duty to prioritize the health of our children based on reality— not conspiracy theories. But now, Trump-appointed Health and Human Services Secretary and conspiracy theorist Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is rolling out his “Make America Healthy Again” (MAHA) report. And it’s as dangerous as it sounds. The report blames “overprescribing” medication for childhood chronic illnesses, lumping in life-saving vaccines and mental health medication for children as bad practices. He’s calling for a full study to review childhood vaccination timelines–something that would completely upend our already safe and effective schedule of vaccines for kids. This is the same anti-vaccine nonsense that RFK Jr. has been peddling for years. As a medical professional, Kim isn't going to be silent as RFK pushes pseudoscience over fact-based medical decisions. Vaccines save lives, full stop. Yet because more and more parents have already fallen victim to the GOP’s fear-mongering tactics, we're seeing once eradicated diseases like measles, mumps, whooping cough, and even polio reemerge with devastating consequences. = It gets worse, Peter: When asked directly in a hearing whether people should take his medical advice, Kennedy admitted: “I don't think people should be taking medical advice from me.” But the problem is—as Health Secretary, people ARE listening to his advice... And right now, his position is to give unscientific ideas a government seal of approval. This isn't just reckless, it's beyond dangerous. RFK Jr. is NOT fit for this job , just like Trump isn’t qualified to be appointing his extremist allies and conspiracy theorists to high-ranking government positions. More than ever before, we need leaders who will stand up for science and protect our families—not those who push pseudoscience and conspiracy theories onto our children. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you, DrKimSchrier.com Rep. Kim Schrier is a doctor, scientist, and mom, fighting for Washington's 8th District in Congress. This is one of the most competitive districts in the country, so we need your help to keep Kim in Congress. name: All On The Line
subject: Anti-democracy lawmakers are trying to make it harder to vote
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND
body:
We can’t sit by and let this happen
|
---

Peter,
Last month, conservatives in the House of Representatives passed the SAVE Act — a bill that would disenfranchise millions of Americans. This extreme, anti-voter bill would make it harder for tens of millions of Americans to make their voices heard by requiring overly burdensome proof of citizenship to register and cast your ballot.
If you think this legislation won’t affect you or someone you love, think again. Tens of millions of legally eligible voters — including military voters, rural voters, tribal citizens, survivors of natural disasters, people of color, working-class Americans, and married women — are all at risk. If you no longer have your original birth certificate or if you changed your name after getting married, for instance, you will have to jump through several time-consuming and costly hurdles just to exercise your basic right to vote.
Extreme lawmakers passed this bill despite widespread opposition from voting rights groups and their constituents — all under the false pretense that it will protect the integrity of our elections.
**Let’s be clear: Making it difficult for certain citizens to vote does not safeguard elections — it actively weakens the very foundation of our democracy.**
All On The Line has been mobilizing supporters to hold the line against this anti-voter law. The Senate is our last chance to stop it from reaching the president’s desk.
**It fell short this week when one senator tried to pass it via unanimous consent, but they are certain to bring it to the floor again. We need to fight back hard to ensure this does not go one step further.**[**Will you make a donation to All On The Line to help protect our democracy from brazen anti-voter attacks like the SAVE Act?**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108254692/547354016/-766669670?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_SAVEActRedux_email_20250523-AOTL-Tier1-SAVEActRedux_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250523-AOTL-Tier1-SAVEActRedux_&utm_campaign=SAVEActRedux&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQ3M2EwYjU4LTE5MzgtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=yk7w4noEkwuAuwF8AHxvMId31O9cz4sSzDYk62ge3_Q=)

name: Research Team [Voter Action Project]
subject: Poll: Barriers to voting
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=25.00&contactdata=prwLFWVH8TOkkDwpSLgmmyciNs61zos9LOkN9g/Vj0y3AUurwTh9fzWXGYxQl1Lb3DKYZAE6uzFRfsbfY50fxy4MoQb2CEa3ITfIb0qNc00=]
Newsweek: How Trans Voters Could Be Excluded From the Election [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA007/1/89215/images/Newsweek_TransgenderVotingBarriers.png]
Nearly 210,000 transgender Americans who were eligible to vote in the 2024 general election faced barriers to voting because of stringent voter ID laws, according to new research.
The study shows that roughly 414,000 voting-eligible transgender Americans lived in the 31 states that primarily conduct their elections in person at the polls and have a voter ID law. Nearly half of transgender voters in those states do not have an ID that correctly reflects their name or gender.
We want to hear from voters with your thoughts. Will you take our quick one-question poll right away?
Should states take steps to improve access to voting for transgender individuals?
By clicking a response below your answer will be automatically recorded:
YES [https://secure.everyaction.com/WI7XQsH6D0Kn6HzC8Tsadw2?am=15.00&contactdata=prwLFWVH8TOkkDwpSLgmmyciNs61zos9LOkN9g/Vj0y3AUurwTh9fzWXGYxQl1Lb3DKYZAE6uzFRfsbfY50fxy4MoQb2CEa3ITfIb0qNc00=] NO [https://secure.everyaction.com/WI7XQsH6D0Kn6HzC8Tsadw2?am=15.00&contactdata=prwLFWVH8TOkkDwpSLgmmyciNs61zos9LOkN9g/Vj0y3AUurwTh9fzWXGYxQl1Lb3DKYZAE6uzFRfsbfY50fxy4MoQb2CEa3ITfIb0qNc00=]
Voter Action Project [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=25.00&contactdata=prwLFWVH8TOkkDwpSLgmmyciNs61zos9LOkN9g/Vj0y3AUurwTh9fzWXGYxQl1Lb3DKYZAE6uzFRfsbfY50fxy4MoQb2CEa3ITfIb0qNc00=]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.

clean_body: Voter Action Project [=] Newsweek: How Trans Voters Could Be Excluded From the Election [] Nearly 210,000 transgender Americans who were eligible to vote in the 2024 general election faced barriers to voting because of stringent voter ID laws, according to new research. The study shows that roughly 414,000 voting-eligible transgender Americans lived in the 31 states that primarily conduct their elections in person at the polls and have a voter ID law. Nearly half of transgender voters in those states do not have an ID that correctly reflects their name or gender. We want to hear from voters with your thoughts. Will you take our quick one-question poll right away? Should states take steps to improve access to voting for transgender individuals? By clicking a response below your answer will be automatically recorded: YES [=] NO [=] Voter Action Project [=] [] [] [] [] Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country. Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities. Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? name: Ruben Gallego
subject: I am holding up Trump’s VA nominees. This is why:
body:
Nobody who uses the VA thinks they’re going to start doing a better job after Trump and Secretary Collins fire 83,000 more staff and take billions out of their budget.
#

No veteran thinks the VA is perfect.
I’d even be willing to bet that everybody working at the VA could list a ton of ways they would improve things if they had a chance.
I saw this firsthand after I came back from Iraq. It wasn’t easy to get the services I needed. A lot of the guys I fought beside struggled with it, too. I had to fight for it for myself and for them.
But I’ll tell you something. No serious person thinks you’re going to cut your way to better services at the VA. Nobody who uses the VA thinks they’re going to start doing a better job after Trump and Secretary Collins fire 83,000 more staff and take billions out of their budget. And at the VA, a ton of those staff are veterans themselves.
It’s absolute nonsense. Completely ridiculous.
I’m not going to give Trump and Collins a pass as they pursue a course of action that is going to do permanent damage to the VA, to the people who work there, and the people who depend on it.
So, yes. I am blocking Collins and Trump’s nominees to the VA. I am going to keep blocking their nominees until they back off their plans to fire all those VA staff.
Some of these appointees may be veterans. They might even be great for the job. But until Collins reverses course and commits to actually working to improve things over at the VA — tough. Because if he can’t do his job without a few political appointees, imagine how much worse it’s going to be when he fires 83,000 staff.
We can’t give these people an inch, and I don’t intend to.
[**Now, I’m asking you: Join me and tell Secretary Collins to reverse course and end his plans to fire 83,000 VA staff.** [TELL COLLINS](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/signup/pet_em_collins/?source=em250523-1-160&akid=3874%2E734936%2EQ5o43t) It’s time to get serious about improving the VA, and this is how we can have a real impact. Thanks for your help, Ruben
